overview: This Pauper deck leverages the Madness mechanic to rapidly discard cards, triggering draw engines and direct damage spells. By cycling through the deck quickly, it aims to overwhelm opponents with early aggression and sustained card advantage. The strategy relies on efficient red removal and low-cost creatures to close games before opponents can stabilize.
win_conditions: Direct damage via Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart
Combat damage from Kessig Flamebreather and other red creatures
Hand disruption through repeated Madness triggers

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one- or two-mana spells
Early Madness outlets to trigger draw effects
A mix of removal and creature threats
mulligan_criteria: Stuck on three-mana or higher cards

early_game: Play cheap creatures to establish board presence
Discard into Lightning Bolt or Lava Dart for immediate impact
Use Madness triggers to draw into follow-up threats
mid_game: Maintain pressure while cycling through the deck
Prioritize discarding into high-value spells or removal
Block efficiently to protect key discard outlets
late_game: Leverage Kessig Flamebreather for sustained card advantage
Close out games with accumulated direct damage
Overwhelm opponent's resources with rapid card draw

key_cards.name: Kessig Flamebreather
key_cards.role: Card advantage engine and primary attacker
key_cards.notes: Discard into this creature or discard other cards while it is on the battlefield to draw cards and maintain board presence.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Use the Madness cost to discard a card and deal three damage, bypassing blockers and accelerating the clock.

key_cards.name: Lava Dart
key_cards.notes: Cast early to trigger Madness effects, dealing two damage while cycling through the deck for better draws.

sequencing_tips: Hold up red mana to pay Madness costs when casting spells
Discard into removal only when necessary to preserve hand size
Prioritize discarding into Kessig Flamebreather to maximize draw potential
Use direct damage spells to finish games rather than overcommitting to combat

matchups.advice: Discard aggressively to deplete their hand and bypass counterspells
Race by playing creatures early and using direct damage to close gaps
Apply pressure before they stabilize with removal and draw

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Mono Red Madness archetype in Pauper format utilizing Kessig Flamebreather, Lightning Bolt, and Lava Dart

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards provide direct damage and discard-to-draw synergy

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General Pauper red aggro sequencing principles applied to Madness mechanics

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Pauper format threats that interact with red aggro strategies
